prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that explains how a point z on the unit circle maps to z squared on a second complex plane. Use animation, a rotating tracker, and staged captions so the doubling of the angle is visually clear.
completion:
from manim import *

class ComplexPlaneSquareMapStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        theta = ValueTracker(0.35)
        title = Text("Squaring Doubles the Angle", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        plane_left = ComplexPlane(x_range=[-2, 2, 1], y_range=[-2, 2, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.28}).add_coordinates().scale(0.75).shift(LEFT * 3.4 + DOWN * 0.2)
        plane_right = ComplexPlane(x_range=[-2, 2, 1], y_range=[-2, 2, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.28}).add_coordinates().scale(0.75).shift(RIGHT * 3.4 + DOWN * 0.2)
        left_circle = ParametricFunction(lambda t: plane_left.n2p(np.cos(t) + 1j * np.sin(t)), t_range=[0, TAU], color=BLUE_D)
        right_circle = ParametricFunction(lambda t: plane_right.n2p(np.cos(t) + 1j * np.sin(t)), t_range=[0, TAU], color=TEAL_D)
        left_label = MathTex("z", color=YELLOW_D).next_to(plane_left, UP)
        right_label = MathTex("z^2", color=ORANGE).next_to(plane_right, UP)
        connector = MathTex("z \mapsto z^2", color=GRAY_B).scale(0.95).move_to(UP * 0.7)
        z_dot = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(plane_left.n2p(np.cos(theta.get_value()) + 1j * np.sin(theta.get_value())), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08))
        z_arrow = always_redraw(lambda: Arrow(plane_left.n2p(0), plane_left.n2p(np.cos(theta.get_value()) + 1j * np.sin(theta.get_value())), buff=0.0, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=5, max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.18))
        z_sq_dot = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(plane_right.n2p(np.cos(2 * theta.get_value()) + 1j * np.sin(2 * theta.get_value())), color=ORANGE, radius=0.08))
        z_sq_arrow = always_redraw(lambda: Arrow(plane_right.n2p(0), plane_right.n2p(np.cos(2 * theta.get_value()) + 1j * np.sin(2 * theta.get_value())), buff=0.0, color=ORANGE, stroke_width=5, max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.18))
        z_trace = TracedPath(z_dot.get_center, stroke_color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4)
        z_sq_trace = TracedPath(z_sq_dot.get_center, stroke_color=ORANGE, stroke_width=4)
        readout = always_redraw(lambda: VGroup(Text("angle", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D), DecimalNumber(theta.get_value() / PI, num_decimal_places=2, font_size=28, color=YELLOW_D), MathTex("\pi", color=YELLOW_D)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.12).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN * 0.55))
        caption_a = Text("The left plane shows z moving once around the unit circle.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("On the right, z squared rotates twice as fast because the angle doubles.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("The magnitude stays one, but the phase evolves much more quickly.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.add(z_trace, z_sq_trace)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane_left), Create(plane_right), Create(left_circle), Create(right_circle), FadeIn(left_label), FadeIn(right_label), FadeIn(connector), run_time=5)
        self.play(FadeIn(z_dot), GrowArrow(z_arrow), FadeIn(z_sq_dot), GrowArrow(z_sq_arrow), FadeIn(readout), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(theta.animate.set_value(PI / 2), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), theta.animate.set_value(PI), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(theta.animate.set_value(3 * PI / 2), run_time=4, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), theta.animate.set_value(2 * PI - 0.2), run_time=4, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that choreographs boolean operations on two overlapping shapes. Animate the source shapes first, then reveal intersection, union, difference, and exclusion as separate beats with clear labels and captions.
completion:
from manim import *

class BooleanOperationsChoreographyStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Boolean Operations Build New Shapes", font_size=35).to_edge(UP)
        left_shape = Circle(radius=1.1, color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.18).shift(LEFT * 1.05)
        right_shape = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.25, width=2.2, height=2.2, color=RED_D, fill_opacity=0.18).shift(RIGHT * 0.85)
        source_group = VGroup(left_shape, right_shape).shift(LEFT * 3.6 + DOWN * 0.2)
        source_label = Text("source shapes", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).next_to(source_group, UP, buff=0.3)
        slots = VGroup(*[Rectangle(width=2.2, height=2.2, stroke_color=GRAY_B, stroke_opacity=0.45) for _ in range(4)]).arrange_in_grid(rows=2, cols=2, buff=0.65).shift(RIGHT * 2.7 + DOWN * 0.25)
        slot_labels = VGroup(
            Text("intersection", font_size=20, color=GREEN_D).next_to(slots[0], UP, buff=0.12),
            Text("union", font_size=20, color=ORANGE).next_to(slots[1], UP, buff=0.12),
            Text("difference", font_size=20, color=PINK).next_to(slots[2], UP, buff=0.12),
            Text("exclusion", font_size=20, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(slots[3], UP, buff=0.12),
        )
        inter_shape = Intersection(left_shape.copy(), right_shape.copy(), color=GREEN_D, fill_opacity=0.45).scale(0.55).move_to(slots[0])
        union_shape = Union(left_shape.copy(), right_shape.copy(), color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.38).scale(0.55).move_to(slots[1])
        diff_shape = Difference(left_shape.copy(), right_shape.copy(), color=PINK, fill_opacity=0.4).scale(0.55).move_to(slots[2])
        excl_shape = Exclusion(left_shape.copy(), right_shape.copy(), color=YELLOW_D, fill_opacity=0.35).scale(0.55).move_to(slots[3])
        source_box = SurroundingRectangle(source_group, color=BLUE_B, buff=0.18)
        caption_a = Text("Start with two overlapping source shapes.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Each boolean operation keeps a different part of the overlap story.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("The same two inputs can generate several distinct geometric results.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(source_group), FadeIn(source_label), Create(source_box), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(slots[0]), FadeIn(slot_labels[0]), TransformFromCopy(source_group, inter_shape), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(slots[1]), FadeIn(slot_labels[1]), TransformFromCopy(source_group, union_shape), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(slots[2]), FadeIn(slot_labels[2]), TransformFromCopy(source_group, diff_shape), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(slots[3]), FadeIn(slot_labels[3]), TransformFromCopy(source_group, excl_shape), run_time=4)
        self.play(AnimationGroup(inter_shape.animate.set_fill(GREEN_D, opacity=0.6), union_shape.animate.set_fill(ORANGE, opacity=0.5), diff_shape.animate.set_fill(PINK, opacity=0.5), excl_shape.animate.set_fill(YELLOW_D, opacity=0.5), lag_ratio=0.12), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene with moving-camera choreography on a phase-plane style spiral trajectory. Start wide on a number plane, follow a probe as it spirals outward, zoom in on local motion, then zoom back out with staged explanatory captions.
completion:
from manim import *

class MovingCameraPhasePlaneStory(MovingCameraScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Following Motion in a Phase Plane", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-5, 5, 1], y_range=[-4, 4, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        spiral = ParametricFunction(lambda t: plane.c2p(0.24 * t * np.cos(t), 0.18 * t * np.sin(t)), t_range=[0.15, 4.3 * PI], color=TEAL_D, stroke_width=5)
        progress = ValueTracker(0.0)
        probe = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(spiral.point_from_proportion(progress.get_value()), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08))
        probe_label = always_redraw(lambda: Text("probe", font_size=20, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(probe, UR, buff=0.12))
        trace = TracedPath(probe.get_center, stroke_color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4)
        frame = self.camera.frame
        highlight = always_redraw(lambda: Circle(radius=0.42, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=3).move_to(probe.get_center()))
        caption_a = Text("The global view shows the overall spiral pattern.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("The moving camera can stay with the probe and magnify local turns.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("Zooming back out reconnects the local motion with the full trajectory.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.add(trace)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), Create(spiral), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(probe), FadeIn(probe_label), progress.animate.set_value(0.34), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(frame.animate.scale(0.55).move_to(probe.get_center()), Create(highlight), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), progress.animate.set_value(0.62), run_time=6, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(progress.animate.set_value(0.84), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(FadeOut(highlight), frame.animate.scale(1.9).move_to(plane.c2p(0, 0)), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), progress.animate.set_value(1.0), run_time=6, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that uses a custom Animation subclass to count upward while the rest of the scene morphs from a small signal into a large signal. Include staged captions, number updates, and a transform-matching style equation transition.
completion:
from manim import *

class Count(Animation):
    def __init__(self, number, start, end, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(number, **kwargs)
        self.start = start
        self.end = end

    def interpolate_mobject(self, alpha):
        value = self.start + alpha * (self.end - self.start)
        self.mobject.set_value(value)

class CustomCounterMorphStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Custom Counter Tracks Growth", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        value = DecimalNumber(0, num_decimal_places=0, font_size=54, color=YELLOW_D).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(UP * 0.8 + LEFT * 0.4)
        value_label = Text("signal level", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).next_to(value, DOWN, buff=0.15)
        eq_small = MathTex("s", "=", "5", color=BLUE_D).scale(1.1).to_edge(LEFT).shift(UP * 1.0)
        eq_large = MathTex("signal", "=", "125", color=ORANGE).scale(1.1).move_to(eq_small)
        chart = BarChart(values=[1.2, 2.0, 3.0], bar_names=["input", "gain", "output"], y_range=[0, 6, 1], y_length=3.6, x_length=5.0).shift(DOWN * 0.7 + LEFT * 0.8)
        bars = chart.bars
        caption_a = Text("A custom animation can drive a numeric readout directly.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("At the same time, the visual story can scale and rename the signal.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("The counter, bars, and equation all reinforce the same transformation.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        callout = SurroundingRectangle(bars[2], color=ORANGE, buff=0.12)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(value), FadeIn(value_label), Write(eq_small), Create(chart), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(Count(value, 0, 40), bars[0].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(1.8), bars[1].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(2.4), bars[2].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(2.8), run_time=4)
        self.play(Count(value, 40, 85), TransformMatchingShapes(eq_small, eq_large), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=5)
        self.play(Count(value, 85, 125), bars[0].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(2.2), bars[1].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(3.1), bars[2].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(4.3), Create(callout), run_time=5)
        self.play(Indicate(callout), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=4)
        self.wait(3)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene on a number line where two moving markers define an interval. Use a live BraceBetweenPoints readout, staged captions, and a small table so the interval widens and narrows as the explanation progresses.
completion:
from manim import *

class BraceIntervalTradeoffStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Intervals Change as the Endpoints Move", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        line = NumberLine(x_range=[0, 12, 1], length=10, include_numbers=True).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        left = ValueTracker(2)
        right = ValueTracker(5)
        left_dot = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(line.n2p(left.get_value()), color=BLUE_D, radius=0.08))
        right_dot = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(line.n2p(right.get_value()), color=ORANGE, radius=0.08))
        interval = always_redraw(lambda: Line(left_dot.get_center(), right_dot.get_center(), color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=6))
        brace = always_redraw(lambda: BraceBetweenPoints(left_dot.get_center(), right_dot.get_center(), direction=UP, color=YELLOW_D))
        width_label = always_redraw(lambda: VGroup(Text("width", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D), DecimalNumber(right.get_value() - left.get_value(), num_decimal_places=1, font_size=28, color=YELLOW_D)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.12).next_to(brace, UP, buff=0.1))
        table = MathTable([["state", "reading"], ["tight", "local focus"], ["medium", "balanced context"], ["wide", "global range"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.36).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        row_tight = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=BLUE_D, buff=0.05)
        row_medium = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.05)
        row_wide = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[3], color=ORANGE, buff=0.05)
        caption_a = Text("The brace reports the live distance between the endpoints.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Wider intervals reveal more context, but they stop feeling local.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("Narrow and wide views each have a role in an explanation.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(line), FadeIn(table), FadeIn(left_dot), FadeIn(right_dot), Create(interval), Create(brace), FadeIn(width_label), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(row_tight), left.animate.set_value(3.0), right.animate.set_value(4.6), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(row_tight, row_medium), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), left.animate.set_value(2.2), right.animate.set_value(6.8), run_time=5)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(row_medium, row_wide), left.animate.set_value(1.0), right.animate.set_value(9.5), run_time=5)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), left.animate.set_value(2.8), right.animate.set_value(7.2), run_time=5)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that explains an acid-base indicator changing across a pH scale. Use a ValueTracker, animated color changes, and staged captions so the transition from acidic to basic is visually clear.
completion:
from manim import *

class AcidBaseIndicatorStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Indicators Shift Color Across the pH Scale", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        scale = NumberLine(x_range=[0, 14, 1], length=10, include_numbers=True).shift(DOWN * 1.1)
        tracker = ValueTracker(2.0)
        beaker = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.18, width=2.8, height=3.2, color=GRAY_B, fill_opacity=0.0).shift(LEFT * 3.0 + UP * 0.3)
        liquid = always_redraw(lambda: Rectangle(width=2.35, height=2.1, stroke_width=0, fill_opacity=0.72, fill_color=interpolate_color(RED_D, BLUE_D, tracker.get_value() / 14)).move_to(beaker.get_center() + DOWN * 0.35))
        marker = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(scale.n2p(tracker.get_value()), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08))
        arrow = always_redraw(lambda: Arrow(marker.get_center() + UP * 0.6, marker.get_center() + UP * 0.02, buff=0.0, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4))
        readout = always_redraw(lambda: VGroup(Text("pH", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D), DecimalNumber(tracker.get_value(), num_decimal_places=1, font_size=30, color=YELLOW_D)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.12).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(UP * 0.8))
        acid_label = Text("acidic", font_size=22, color=RED_D).next_to(scale.n2p(1.5), DOWN, buff=0.25)
        neutral_label = Text("neutral", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D).next_to(scale.n2p(7), DOWN, buff=0.25)
        base_label = Text("basic", font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).next_to(scale.n2p(12.5), DOWN, buff=0.25)
        caption_a = Text("At low pH, the indicator reads as acidic and stays warm-colored.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Near neutral, the color transitions through the middle of the scale.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("By high pH, the same liquid has shifted fully into a basic color range.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(scale), FadeIn(acid_label), FadeIn(neutral_label), FadeIn(base_label), Create(beaker), FadeIn(liquid), FadeIn(marker), GrowArrow(arrow), FadeIn(readout), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(6.8), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=6)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(11.8), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=6)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(8.2), run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that shows an electron in a Bohr-style atom absorbing energy and jumping to a higher orbit. Use orbit transitions, a photon arrow, and staged captions so the excitation story is easy to follow.
completion:
from manim import *

class BohrExcitationTransitionStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("An Electron Jump Signals Excitation", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        nucleus = Dot(ORIGIN, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.14)
        orbit_low = Circle(radius=1.1, color=BLUE_D)
        orbit_high = Circle(radius=1.9, color=TEAL_D)
        electron = Dot(orbit_low.point_at_angle(PI / 6), color=BLUE_E, radius=0.08)
        low_label = MathTex("n=1", color=BLUE_D).next_to(orbit_low, RIGHT)
        high_label = MathTex("n=2", color=TEAL_D).next_to(orbit_high, RIGHT)
        photon = Arrow(LEFT * 3.8 + UP * 0.8, LEFT * 1.6 + UP * 0.3, color=ORANGE, stroke_width=5)
        photon_label = Text("energy in", font_size=22, color=ORANGE).next_to(photon, UP, buff=0.12)
        glow = SurroundingRectangle(photon_label, color=ORANGE, buff=0.16)
        caption_a = Text("The electron starts on the lower orbit close to the nucleus.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("An incoming photon supplies the energy needed for a jump.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("After excitation, the electron occupies a larger orbit farther out.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(nucleus), Create(orbit_low), FadeIn(electron), FadeIn(low_label), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(GrowArrow(photon), FadeIn(photon_label), Create(glow), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=4)
        jumped_electron = electron.copy().move_to(orbit_high.point_at_angle(PI / 5))
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(orbit_low.copy(), orbit_high), FadeIn(high_label), ReplacementTransform(electron, jumped_electron), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), FadeOut(photon), FadeOut(photon_label), FadeOut(glow), run_time=6)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(jumped_electron, orbit_high), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that starts with mixed points in a latent space and then animates them into clearer clusters. Use point movement, cluster highlights, and transition captions so the embedding separation becomes obvious.
completion:
from manim import *

class LatentSpaceClusterTransitionStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Latent Space Organizes into Clusters", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], x_length=7.0, y_length=5.0, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 0.6 + DOWN * 0.15)
        mixed_positions = [(-1.6, 0.5), (-1.1, -0.1), (-0.8, 0.8), (0.2, 0.0), (0.6, 0.6), (1.0, -0.4), (1.7, 0.5), (1.2, 1.0)]
        target_positions = [(-2.1, 1.0), (-1.8, 0.2), (-2.3, 0.5), (0.3, -0.2), (0.8, -0.5), (0.6, 0.3), (2.2, 0.8), (2.5, 0.2)]
        colors = [BLUE_D, BLUE_D, BLUE_D, ORANGE, ORANGE, ORANGE, TEAL_D, TEAL_D]
        dots = VGroup(*[Dot(axes.c2p(x, y), color=color, radius=0.08) for (x, y), color in zip(mixed_positions, colors)])
        moved_dots = VGroup(*[Dot(axes.c2p(x, y), color=color, radius=0.08) for (x, y), color in zip(target_positions, colors)])
        cluster_boxes = VGroup(
            SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(*moved_dots[:3]), color=BLUE_D, buff=0.18),
            SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(*moved_dots[3:6]), color=ORANGE, buff=0.18),
            SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(*moved_dots[6:]), color=TEAL_D, buff=0.18),
        )
        caption_a = Text("At first, the representation looks noisy and partially mixed.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("As training shapes the embedding, related points drift together.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("Cluster highlights make the separated structure easy to read.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), FadeIn(dots), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(*[ReplacementTransform(dot, moved) for dot, moved in zip(dots, moved_dots)], ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=6)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[Create(box) for box in cluster_boxes], lag_ratio=0.18), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)
